opp.name: Nexteer_S2P_2025
opp.next_step: June 12 Rick: Exec Champion says they've completed the Medix sales cycle for Direct, next up is Indirect. Miscommunication between Indirect champion (Marshall) and Exec Champion (Chris Bratt) - they need to meet internally today, figure out how they want to proceed.
opp.ais_why:
• Multiple Nexteer buyers pulled the conversation toward AI themselves. Global Purchasing Director Chris Bratt asked: "do the vendors engage in an AI conversation quite openly, or does it turn them off?" Call notes record that the autonomous AI negotiation and AP Smartdesk capabilities impressed the attendees, and Zycus was actioned to send AI negotiation case studies to Chris.
• The buyer also articulated AI-specific need language: enhanced automation for supplier onboarding, risk monitoring, and end-to-end sourcing event management, including autonomous negotiation for ad hoc purchases. Zycus has AI flagged as Very Critical for the account, and the in-scope product list explicitly includes Agentic AI.
• Why not Hungry: BANT is incomplete with budget still being built (a protected number for next year), Krzysztof Mekwinski's deepest concerns were ERP/QAD integration and three-way match, and Karolina Cisek's questions focused on invoice OCR and cycle times. With no AI POC committed yet, this sits at the cautious end of AI Curious.

items.source: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (Chris Bratt on Jacky Xu)

items.quote: we can get that data from each of the regions, Marshall, can we?

items.area: No transcribed buyer working session in 2026
items.source: Avoma manifest: last transcribed call 14 Nov 2025

sources: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (Chris Bratt on regional spend data)
Value Engineering / Business Case call 2025-11-14 and 2025-08-01 ($4-5M savings)
metrics.narrative: The value case is quantified but still Zycus-owned rather than buyer-validated. Arthur (Zycus VE lead) sized roughly $4-5M year-one savings on tail spend alone against about $500M indirect spend (14 Nov 2025 and 1 Aug 2025 value-engineering calls), and Chris Bratt confirmed Nexteer has no single global system and that each region pulls spend differently ('every region has its Power BIs...but it just might be in a slightly different format for each region', 10 Sep 2025). The gap is that Nexteer never returned the region-by-region spend extract Arthur repeatedly requested, so the ROI remains a modeled estimate, not a jointly built number the buyer co-owns. Making the buyer validate the savings model is the move that turns Metrics from partial to confirmed.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Marshall L. Bonner (Evaluator), Chris Bratt (Decision Maker)
champion.narrative: There are two champions, both operationally bought in but cooling. Marshall L. Bonner (Procurement Manager, SF Evaluator) is the Indirect champion who drove the supplier/invoicing demo asks, the ROI transparency requirement, and the user-count and data-gathering work across 2025, and is named as the Indirect champion in the 12 Jun 2026 next step. Chris Bratt (Global Commodity and Product Line Purchasing Director, SF Decision Maker) is the exec champion and quarterback with EB access. What is missing is active advocacy: neither has introduced Jacky Xu, and Chris has not scheduled the promised EB meeting in 9+ months. The 12 Jun 2026 miscommunication between Marshall and Chris on Indirect sequencing shows the two are not aligned, and Marshall has driven no working session since late 2025. A strong champion would have resolved the sequencing question and escalated to the EB by now; rebuilding Marshall's active advocacy ranks before any commercial step.

sources: Task 2026-05-15 (Mendex supplier module for all suppliers)
Value Engineering call 2025-11-14 (Zycus disqualified from Direct; Indirect deferral)
competition.narrative: The live competitive threat is Medix (referred to as 'Mendex', a Siemens solution) plus the risk of deferral. Nexteer completed the Medix sales cycle for Direct materials (Next Step 12 Jun 2026), which is fine on its own because Zycus disqualified itself from Direct, but the 15 May 2026 activity note is the real risk: 'they plan to use Mendex's Supplier module for all direct/indirect suppliers.' That intent lets Medix creep into the Indirect scope Zycus is counting on. The second threat is status-quo/deferral: Eric Chavez said Indirect is 'more like a year away, tied to our ERP activities' (14 Nov 2025). Zycus wins by making Indirect a parallel quick-win ($4-5M year-one tail-spend savings) with Merlin AI auto-negotiation and true global standardization that a Direct-materials supplier module cannot match, decoupled from the Direct/ERP timeline.

sources: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (spot buy, invoice-status gap)
identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is specific and buyer-articulated. Nexteer has no global spend visibility (regional Power BI silos, no spend cube), pervasive maverick/spot-buy behavior with no catalog contracts ('almost everything here is a spot buy...if you want a widget, you get a quote', Marshall 10 Sep 2025), and a broken invoice-status loop where suppliers get no acknowledgement ('there is no reliable communication path...the supplier does not know that his invoice was accepted, he only knows if it's rejected', Marshall 10 Sep 2025), forcing buyers to manually field payment-status queries. Manual, region-inconsistent invoice matching and onboarding drive cycle-time and effort cost. The pain owner is Marshall Bonner operationally and Chris Bratt strategically (global standardization mandate).

sources: Budget call 2025-07-07 (5-year term, pricing to September)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are only lightly scoped. Nexteer uses the PO as its contract for indirect ('IPO is my contract...everything in the US is a spot buy order', Marshall 10 Sep 2025) and holds few framework agreements, so paper is simpler than a typical enterprise deal. Legal is handled largely in-house with one or two counsel per region (Jonas and Pete in the US, plus Poland, China and Mexico local lawyers, 10 Sep 2025). Zycus proposed a standard 5-year term with pricing held to September (budget call 2025-07-07), and the buyer flagged a potential ERP move from QAD toward SAP HANA (Art 14 Nov 2025) as an integration dependency. No MSA/SOW redline, procurement channel, or InfoSec review has begun; a mutual close/paper plan tied to the Indirect scope would create the missing visibility.

sources: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (Chris Bratt on Jacky Xu)
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is mapped by name and title but has never been engaged. Jacky Xu (VP, Chief Purchasing Officer, Global Supply Management) is the Salesforce Executive Sponsor and controls the global standardization mandate and budget, but he has not attended a single call. Chris Bratt (Decision Maker) is the gate: on the 10 Sep 2025 value call he said Jacky 'is a believer' and 'quite visionary when it comes to this sort of stuff' but 'he's gonna be looking for me and my team to make a recommendation, and at that stage I think he'll start to engage.' Budget is being built as a protected number for next year (SF AIS_Why__c). So budget authority is understood but no direct EB access exists; securing a path to Jacky Xu once Chris's team recommends is the open lever.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a recommendation-then-approval motion currently blocked internally. Nexteer ran discovery, a value-engineering/ROI study, and budgetary pricing through 2025, with Chris Bratt as the acknowledged quarterback ('you've been our quarterback the whole time', Rick 10 Sep 2025). The plan is for Chris and his team to make a recommendation, after which Jacky Xu engages for sign-off, timed to budget season (referenced as around August). The process has since fractured: on 12 Jun 2026 the exec champion reported the Medix Direct cycle is complete and Indirect is 'next', but there is a miscommunication between Marshall (Indirect champion) and Chris Bratt on how to sequence it, unresolved. The near-term process question is whether Indirect proceeds now or is deferred behind ERP work, not which vendor.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well documented from the workshops and an RFP. Nexteer wants one global unified S2P platform to replace fragmented regional systems ('one global system where we pull all of that from in a consistent way', Chris Bratt 10 Sep 2025), multilingual support (English, Polish, Chinese, Spanish per Alexa and April, 25 Jun 2025), automated OCR invoice processing and 2-way/3-way matching (April: US/Mexico 2-way, rest of world 3-way), supplier onboarding and risk monitoring (Iris), and AI-driven auto-negotiation of unmanaged tail spend (Ronit 1 Aug 2025). Zycus explicitly disqualified itself from Direct materials (APQP/PPAP/PPAP-doc workflows 'not a play for us...this feels very much like an MRP product lifecycle requirement', Art 14 Nov 2025), so the criteria that matter for Zycus are the Indirect/global-standardization and AI set, all confirmed on calls.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Nexteer Automotive Group Limited — Qualified · $785K · closes 20 Nov

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 23 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 8 is anchored on the stage win (23), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is cooling. After a strong 2025 discovery and value-engineering run, the deal regressed on 9 Jun 2026 (stage backward to Qualified, forecast cut to Pipeline), Direct went to Medix, and the Indirect path is stuck on an unresolved internal Marshall/Chris alignment issue; the only 2026 buyer contact is email banter and a social lunch.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is thinning: no transcribed buyer working session since 14 Nov 2025, only email banter and a social lunch in 2026, plus a stage regression and forecast downgrade on 9 Jun 2026.

north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-11-20 is 140 days out. Deal created and qualified 25 Mar 2025 and has sat in Qualified for 460+ days with no submission-date milestones recorded, so stage velocity is effectively zero. On 9 Jun 2026 the stage was pushed backward (Shortlisted to Qualified) and the forecast cut (Upside Key Deal to Pipeline). Last real buyer touch was a social lunch with Chris Bratt on 14-15 May 2026 (49 days ago); the 12 Jun 2026 next step is a rep note of an unresolved internal Nexteer blocker with no buyer reply since. To hit 20 Nov the buyer must still run Indirect ShoeFit/use-case validation, commercials, ROI sign-off, negotiation and contracting; at current pace the date is not credible but the primary problem is stalled momentum, not just timing.
evidence: Field history 2026-06-09: StageName Shortlisted to Qualified and ForecastCategoryName Upside Key Deal to Pipeline (by Rick Taranek) - a regression, not progress.

Next Step 2026-06-12: 'they've completed the Medix sales cycle for Direct, next up is Indirect. Miscommunication between Indirect champion (Marshall) and Exec Champion (Chris Bratt) - they need to meet internally today.'
LastActivityDate 2026-05-15 was a social lunch ('great to catch-up') with Chris Bratt, not a working session; no transcribed buyer call since 14 Nov 2025.

Economic Buyer Jacky Xu (VP, CPO) has never appeared on a call; Chris Bratt 10 Sep 2025: 'he'll be looking for me and my team to make a recommendation, and at that stage I think he'll start to engage.'
north_star_verdict.headline: Momentum is fading: the deal regressed from Shortlisted to Qualified and was downgraded to Pipeline on 9 Jun 2026, Direct went to Medix, and the Indirect play is stalled on an unresolved internal Marshall and Chris Bratt sequencing conflict with no buyer working session since 2025.

flags: Counter Mendex creeping into Indirect: differentiate Zycus AI/Merlin tail-spend auto-negotiation and global standardization before Nexteer extends its Direct supplier module to indirect (buyer stated intent 15 May 2026).
Economic buyer Jacky Xu has never engaged; convert Marshall's operational buy-in and Chris Bratt's quarterback role into a path to the EB once the team recommendation forms.
Reverse the 9 Jun 2026 regression: the deal slipped Shortlisted to Qualified and forecast to Pipeline; re-establish a dated mutual Indirect evaluation plan to restore momentum.

Zycus is not competing on Direct (self-disqualified on APQP/PPAP, Art 14 Nov 2025), so the fight is Indirect + global standardization + AI. The second threat is status-quo/deferral, with Eric Chavez placing Indirect 'a year away, tied to ERP activities' (14 Nov 2025). • The buyer also articulated AI-specific need language: enhanced automation for supplier onboarding, risk monitoring, and end-to-end sourcing event management, including autonomous negotiation for ad hoc purchases. Zycus has AI flagged as Very Critical for the account, and the in-scope product list explicitly includes Agentic AI.
• Why not Hungry: BANT is incomplete with budget still being built (a protected number for next year), Krzysztof Mekwinski's deepest concerns were ERP/QAD integration and three-way match, and Karolina Cisek's questions focused on invoice OCR and cycle times. With no AI POC committed yet, this sits at the cautious end of AI Curious.
